metadata.subject: public_order_ethics
metadata.heading: intelligence collection in law & order situations:-

page_content: instead of making efforts to solve law and order problem, if advance information is collected about the problem, preventive measures can be taken. fatigue, wastage of time and human resources can be avoided. similarly, resentment and unpopularity from the public can be checked. if there is a law and order problem, immediately the persons and reasons for the problem should be identified and it should be resolved. information of the following should be collected:

metadata.subject: public_order_ethics
metadata.heading: intelligence collection in law & order situations:-

page_content: agitations: different sections of society will try to bring pressure on the government through agitations, to resolve their demands.
note : this topic is dealt in law and order subject in this book.

industrial labour - unions: trade unions will always resort to agitations to solve the problems of labour. to prevent such agitations, following information should be collected, which is useful for police:

1. police should know the number of factories, strength of employees / workers, their affiliations to political groups and their leaders, in their jurisdiction. 2. internal dissensions within the union should also be monitored. 3. relations of the union leaders with the management. 4. scope of declaring strikes, lockouts and others. 5. conflict with the managements and the destruction of the property. 6. by the collection of advance information on the above mentioned points and analyzing it, preventive measures can be taken.
agricultural labour and farmers: in recent times, we have been observing agricultural labour and farmers also resorting to agitations for various reasons and causing law and order problems.

ex:power tariff, compensation to families of farmers who committed suicides, increase of daily wages, remunerative price to agricultural produce, controlling the prices of fertilizers, seeds, pesticides and other things needed for farming. during such agitations, we must find out the main reason for the agitation.

the antecedents of the person leading the agitation and his intention should be ascertained. generally, the agitators will get banners and placards for agitation through painters and artists. police can know the programme from painters and artists. similarly, the information can be known from printing presses, where pamphlets are being printed. particulars of political and communal leaders who give instigating/provocating speeches should be collected. some agitations take place purely for publicity. in that case, information about agitators can be known from media, in advance. by maintaining cordial relations with press and media, collection of information becomes easier. some information can also be collected from the victimized group, which is put to loss, due to agitation.

nowadays terrorism is a universal problem. many countries are suffering from the activities of the terrorists. due to the divergent views in different sections of the public, some take part in terrorist activities. terrorists/ extremists are getting the most sophisticated weapons. they are getting these weapons either from local manufacturers or purchase from other countries. local weapon manufacturers are selling the weapons to terrorists and extremists. terrorists are purchasing raw material of explosives from open market and securing from explosive factories and manufacturing the bombs, to cause damage and destruction to public and government property and to kill common people and government employees. they use landmines on a large scale to kill police personnel.

extremists and terrorists obtain dynamite from coal mines and commit offences. by colluding with the contractors of explosives and tampering the records, some of them are resorting to anti social activities. under such circumstances, police should establish broad information network system, collect information and take necessary, appropriate action. the information regarding the following points should be collected:

1. areas of extremists ' domination. 2. activities of extremists group, their affiliated political parties, their squad leaders and cadres. 3. particulars of the offences committed by extremists and the methods of committing crimes. 4. opinions and apprehensions of public. 5. sympathizers. 6. their place of residence.

7. particulars of youth, students who are missing/ absconding, and incidents of their joining extremists groups. 8. particulars of arms, ammunition explosives and its raw material. 9. knowledge of explosive dealers in their respective police jurisdiction.

metadata.subject: public_order_ethics
metadata.heading: political parties - their affiliated associations (frontal organizations):

page_content: advance information should be collected about the activities and agitations of political parties and necessary preventives steps should be taken. generally, political parties and their frontal organizations conduct the following activities:-

metadata.subject: public_order_ethics
metadata.heading: political parties - their affiliated associations (frontal organizations):

page_content: 1. meetings 2. processions 3. dharnas 4. bandhs 5. gherao 6. rasta roko (road blockade) and hunger strikes.

metadata.subject: public_order_ethics
metadata.heading: political parties - their affiliated associations (frontal organizations):

page_content: sometimes, small incidents turn into major violent incidents and cause danger to the lives and property of people. police should collect advance information on the political parties, understand the issues and try to preserve law and order. advance information should be collected on the strength of the parties, founders or its trouble making leaders and the likely incidents going to happen.
note: this topic is dealt under the subject 'law and order ' .

factional feuds: factions are formed due to desire for dominance in the villages. due to old enmity, factions and groups are formed. collection of information on the following points will be very much useful in preventing the formation of factional feuds:

1. identifying the villages which have factional feuds. 2. strength of each faction leader. 3. the recent and old factional feuds. 4. procurement of arms, ammunition and explosives. 5. probable causes, irritating points for frictions. 6. keeping vigil on illegal way of making money and its sources.
ex: manufacture and sale of illicit liquor, organizing gambling houses etc.
information should be collected on these points and precautionary measures should be taken.
during elections, there is every possibility of friction, among the parties. so, advance information should be collected on the following aspects and following preventive steps should be taken:-

1. nature of disputes or difference of opinion between the ruling and opposition parties. 2. the thinking of people of the particular area. 3. strength and existence of regional and national parties 4. strength of each important leader in the party. 5. nature of friction and riots that took place in the previous elections. 6. particulars of the persons who committed the snatching of ballot boxes. 7. particulars of the persons who tried to tear away/ soil the ballot papers. 8. caste and communal frictions. 9. post elections situation of the village.

examinations: in the present educational system, examinations play key role. different universities, boards and commissions conduct examinations at different levels. advance information should be collected on examination centers and persons who resort to malpractices, incidents of leakage of question papers. by coordinating with the boards and wings of the examinations, effective preventive measures should taken.
liaison with other wings of administration: in the collection of information the cooperation of other wings of administration is very much essential. so, police must have cordial relations with them.

metadata.subject: public_order_ethics
metadata.heading: information should also be collected from the following sources:

page_content: 1. from passport office, particulars of passport holders. 2. from immigration office, information regarding the specific purpose and durations of visits of the foreigners should be collected. purpose of persons going aboard, from india can be known. information should be collected about the scrutiny that takes place before the issue of passport. 3. information should also be collected from airport/airlines authorities and travel agencies. 4. by maintaining cordial relations with some foreign voluntary organizations, collection of information will be easier. 5. the particulars of passport applicants should be thoroughly verified else this may lead to security problem of the country. 6. all the hotels and lodges within the jurisdictions of the police stations shall be checked for proper entry of the particulars of foreign guests, frequently.

metadata.subject: public_order_ethics
metadata.heading: information should also be collected from the following sources:

page_content: 7. similarly, efforts should be made to have close liaison with the other departments, like, revenue, transport, medical and health and jails, for collection of information about the offences/offenders.
apart from the above issues, information shall also be collected about the following:-

generally, anti-social elements, factionists and illegal animal hunters will be in need of illegal arms and ammunition. these people will mostly use local made guns. vigil should be kept on the lathe machines which manufacture iron articles and other material, required for guns. information should be collected about the transporters/suppliers of illegal arms and ammunition. a watch should be kept on the licensed arms and explosive dealers within the jurisdiction of police station.

these are in abundance and everywhere. especially, during election the political parties will supply liquor and non veg food, along with money, to maintain their followers. in this process, much preference will be given to spurious liquor and country made arrack. sometimes, there will be much loss of life (in hundreds), due to consumption of spurious liquor. vigil should be kept on country arrack distilleries and spurious liquor manufacturing units. by proper coordination with the departments of excise, forest., revenue and weights and measures, the raw material and transport information should be obtained, and checked and illegally stocked material should be destroyed by taking suitable action against the persons responsible for it.

the offenders spend the easy earned money for vices. one of them is gambling. organizing gambling houses, making financial transactions with the persons in gambling houses, resorting to fights and frictions after losing money, is a common scene in gambling houses. police should collect information on such places of gambling, its supporters and the available routes to reach that place. this will result in tracing the offenders in other crimes also. if gambling houses are encouraged, it will increase the rate of theft. police should keep a special watch on gambling houses during fairs and festivals.

human trafficking and prostitution are the offences which are inter connected. some offenders do trafficking of children and girls. children are used for begging, to work in houses of the rich people and use them as jockeys in horse and camel races in arab countries. in some cases, parents or nearest relatives will sell away their children due to poverty. sometimes, some gangs will kidnap and sell the children. young ladies are lured with false promises and sold to brothel houses. sometimes, young ladies themselves enter into prostitution, due to poverty. human trafficking will also be done for the sake of internal organs, like kidney and liver etc

even though the computers are being used since decades, the law implementing agencies have not extended their area of operations to computer related crimes. increase in computer literacy, use of computers at houses, offices, commercial centers, and banks and their link to online operations, have increased cyber offences.

in recent days cyber crime offences with latest technology are posing a serious threat. they are different from traditional offences of persons and property. easy availability of computers and time has become encouraging factors to white collar offenders. due to lack of efficient investigators in this field, majority of the high tech offences are going undetected. so the indian government has brought in the comprehensive information technology act 2000, into force. this act enables the police to deal firmly with the cyber offenders. for the implementation of this act, police have established special cyber offences cells, to get the information about cyber offences.

the comprehensive meaning of cyber crime is logging with fraudulent intention into a net work (hacking) making changes by defacing/deleting/inserting/stealing content or data in the targeted system, network, program and data. the criminals target institutions, banks, individuals. many a time they get away with the victims money in the process of online banking or e-commerce. they use data to cheat or misappropriate properties with fraudulent means. terrorist use internet, social media for propagating their tailored misinformation to lure vulnerable youth, recruiting, training, procuring resources perpetrating crimes etc.,

recently, cyber forensic science branch has under taken the task of retrieving the removed and stolen information, eliminating the virus from the information, reviving the useful information, finding out the origin of e-mail and steganographic images. for this purpose, if the offence is identified by the victims, the computer should not be tampered. experts will copy and analyze the information through special software under 'bit system ' . through this, the digital evidence can be produced in court of law, without any controversy and ensure, that it is intact.

observing the movements of enemy agents who come to collect the secret information and the organizations which are working against the interest and security of the country in disguise, is called counter intelligence. this is also called collection of intelligence from enemy agents, who are engaged in collecting information from us. counter intelligence is not only a sensitive, but also a complicated task.

metadata.subject: public_order_ethics
metadata.heading: counter intelligence on various terrorists groups and methodology adopted for collection of intelligence to avert untoward incidents:

page_content: collecting information and nullifying the activities of the enemy agents engaged in our country, for collection of information and espionage, is the main aim of counter intelligence. all foreign spying agents engaged in india have four main objectives. they are here to know the political situation, foreign policies, capacity of defense forces, issues of science and technological development and to a certain socio economic coalitions. apart from this, they try to defame their opponents through bad propaganda, for their selfish ends. they will try to destroy political system, weaken the organizations and causing hurdles for them.

1. to identify the enemy intelligence and espionage activities of groups, in india. 2. efforts to check out the activities of enemy agents and neutralize their plan of destruction. 3. deactivating the enemy intelligence organizations. 4. appointing some reliable persons as secret agents in foreign countries. 5. collection of information from the car drivers of foreigners and staff of foreign embassies. 6. formation of shadow parties and action teams, to counter the activities of foreign agents, militants and extremists, by watching their movements.

if law and order is maintained, people will lead a peaceful life. it is necessary for police to collect advance information. at present, society is studded with regionalism, communalism, casteism, political friction, terrorism and extremism. to balance all these problems and for effective functioning of police, to preserve law and order and to take security measures, collection of correct advanced intelligence is necessary.

video surveillance, often referred to as closed-circuit television (cctv) or simply surveillance cameras, is a technology used to monitor and record activities in a specific area or location. it has applications in various sectors, including security, law enforcement and public safety. here's an overview of video surveillance:

1. cameras: surveillance cameras capture video footage of the monitored area. they come in various types, including fixed cameras, pan-tilt-zoom (ptz) cameras, and hidden cameras. 2. recording and storage: the video feed is recorded and stored on digital video recorders (dvrs) or network video recorders (nvrs). these devices allow for the long-term storage of footage, which can be accessed for review or investigation. 3. monitoring and control: video feeds can be monitored in real-time by security personnel or automated systems. control centers are equipped with screens to view multiple camera feeds simultaneously. 4. communication and alarms: video surveillance systems can be integrated with communication systems and alarms. this enables security personnel to respond to incidents promptly. 5. analytics and software: modern video surveillance systems often use advanced analytics and software for functions like facial recognition, license plate recognition, and motion detection. these features help automate the monitoring process and can trigger alerts based on specific criteria. 6. network connectivity: many surveillance systems are networked, allowing authorized personnel to access video feeds remotely. this is useful for off-site monitoring and investigations.

1. security: video surveillance is widely used to enhance security in various settings, including homes, businesses, public spaces, and critical infrastructure. it helps deter and detect criminal activities. 2. law enforcement: police departments use surveillance cameras to monitor public areas, identify suspects, and gather evidence in criminal investigations. 3. traffic management: surveillance cameras are used for traffic monitoring, enforcing traffic laws, and managing traffic flow.
4. public safety: surveillance systems in public spaces, such as airports, train stations, and stadiums, help ensure public safety by monitoring crowds and identifying potential threats.
video surveillance can be a valuable tool for enhancing security and public safety.

metadata.subject: public_order_ethics
metadata.heading: general principles of vip security including asl, control and asc

page_content: vip protection involves providing safety and security to high profile dignitaries and their family members. in recent times the threat to the security of vips has increased sharply on account of various activities of terrorists and other hostile elements. access to modern technology, sophisticated weapons, sophisticated improvised explosive devices (ieds), drones and new innovative tactics have increased the danger manifold. in india, security details are provided to high-risk dignitaries/individuals by state police and other security agencies. depending upon the threat perception of the dignitary/ individual, the level of security is categorised, which may vary in terms of psos, escort teams, type of motor fleet and level of coordination required with other agencies.

metadata.subject: public_order_ethics
metadata.heading: general principles of vip security including asl, control and asc

page_content: a seamless coordination with various sister agencies is the key factor in vip security arrangements, any failure would be detrimental to the dignitary and will give a poor name to the security providing organization as well as to the country. the current volatile global security situation demands that vip security arrangements and the overall aspects in this subject should be dealt with, thoughtfully and precisely.

metadata.subject: public_order_ethics
metadata.heading: general principles of vip security including asl, control and asc

page_content: detailed information about an intended or potential target is the first step in any terrorist plan of violence against an individual. the protected person and the family members should, therefore, ensure that information regarding their travel plan, location, personal habits and traits should be disclosed only on 'need to know' basis. however, this does not mean that the information cannot be obtained by a determined evil mind, but this kind of precaution does put up inhibiting barriers making any attempt to acquire such specific information noticeable, thus providing a potential alert mechanism for the security authorities.

vip is a person holding high office and who is in need of protective arrangements by virtue of the office he or she holds, thus this term would cover heads of states, ministers and others of such status. sometimes it may become necessary to make special protective arrangements to eminent personalities, who may not hold any public office. political leaders, govt. officials and industrialists are increasingly becoming the target of attack from terrorists, naxalites, organized crime, lone wolf attacks, radicalized individuals due to popular perception that they have the capacity to influence political decisions and policies. such attacks are intended to destabilize the govt. and to create fear among the public.

presently, states and central security agencies have their own security wings with varying organizational structure, induction process, training modules, on-the-job refresher training, training facilities, de-induction process and security protocol/drills. being controlled by different agencies, vip security in india lacks proper co- ordination among agencies. often, the central agencies mandated for vip security, face difficulties in coordination, matching professionalism and use of latest technology with state vip security agencies leading to security lapses sometimes.
- threat perception - access control - anti-sabotage check - advance security liaison - defense in depth - contingency planning

prevention is always better than cure. the authorities responsible for the protection of the vip, should organize an effective system of intelligence collection pertaining to threat to their protectee. they should have information on the activities, strength, capabilities of the group/ persons hostile to the vip and keep the activities of such group(s) under surveillance. the security agencies should organize their protective intelligence system in such a way that well documented and verified data on potential threats is available to them for consultation and action when the need arises.
threat assessment is based on

- i) inputs from the field 2. ii) tech inputs 3. iii) interrogation reports 4. iv) mac/ smac sharing (threat inputs) - v) area survey reports 6. vi) sister agencies/ state police 7. vii) strategic value of the vip 8. viii) controversy/ rivalries related to the vip 9. ix) tough decisions taken by the vip - x) the adversaries may benefit by eliminating the vip
- xi) revenge (declared/ perceived) - xii) l & o scenario
xiii) threatening mails/ letters
xiv) data bank

access control allows movement of authorized personnel, vehicles and materials at authorized time, into and out of a building/complex where vvip is located or will visit. key to successful access control is the integration of security personnel, procedures and equipment into a system that protects the vvip from all external and internal threats.
access control of visitors and vehicles

- i) pre-appointment (need to know basis) 2. ii) positive confirmation of identity 3. iii) registration of name/ time of entry 4. iv) dfmd/ hhmd (obtrusive and unobtrusive) - v) checking of materials carried 6. vi) holding area 7. vii) communication to all concerned
viii) time of exit

- ix) strong perimeter walls & gates - x) slow down measures (barriers, speed breakers, staggered concrete flower pots) - xi) stopping vehicle & checking of vehicles. - xii) check the vehicle pass, identity of the visitor/ driver
xiii) communicate with concerned
anti-sabotage check is a systematic check/ search of a designated area by technically qualified people with technical equipment and sniffer dogs. the aim is to make the area sterile and safe.

all elevation surrounding the residence, high rise buildings overlooking the same, underground sewers which may facilitate ingress into the building, cellars, false ceilings etc. should be thoroughly checked and covered by protective arrangements. before the arrival of the vip and his entourage, the entire complex should be searched for explosives and such devices of sabotage and all unused rooms, cellars etc. should be sealed after search and kept under watch. this anti sabotage check should be conducted daily, preferably when the vip is out. all electrical gadgets and appliances in the building should be subjected to check by competent technicians to rule out scope for sabotage through them. sources of electric energy and water supply are to be checked and guarded. alternate standby arrangements should be kept ready.

a number of agencies are involved in the arrangements for the vvip, when he attends a function or attends an engagement outside his residence. it is necessary for the concerned security agency, the organizers of the function and the government agencies involved in the function to conduct asl. asl provides a forum of coordination connected with security arrangements so that all agencies concerned work in unison with perfect understanding of each other's roles and responsibilities. in this connection before the actual visit or function, a team of security, intelligence and protocol officers do an advance visit of the venue along with organizers to assess the security requirements.

defense in depth implies deployment of multi layers of security between the protectee and the potential threat. this system is termed as protective security cordons. generally three cordons are deployed by most of the security agencies.

the purpose of these layers of security/cordons is to ensure that only authorized and most vouchsafed persons are able to reach the proximity of the protective. this system helps in filtering of men and materials and for confronting the threat at various points so that even if a failure takes place at one stage, detection of the threat becomes possible at a subsequent stage, thus ensuring defense in depth.

contingency planning is an integral part of security planning. contingency plan is devised for specific situations when things are not going as planned/expected affecting normal activity, safety/security of vvip. contingency can be of two types:

- a. protection drills during formation, walkabout, car cade etc. - b. safe houses/ shelters (accessibility and defend ability) - c. contingency exits - d. contingency car cades - e. contingency routes
- a. contingency medical care schemes - b. medical care: on the spot, enroute, definitive care - c. static and mobile medical teams - d. contingency ambulance - e. contingency hospital

metadata.subject: public_order_ethics
metadata.heading: vip security by different agencies in india

page_content: vip security is planned and categorized on the basis of threat, perception. apart from spg, central security agencies which are engaged in vip security duties are nsg, crpf, cisf & itbp. also, as per security guidelines book, it is the responsibility of the states/uts to provide security to the vip within whose jurisdiction, for the time being, the vip happens to be present. as per mha guidelines and yellow book, vip security is categorized and classified as under:

spg, strength of pso's & convoy/ carcade = classified. z +, strength of pso's & convoy/ carcade = depends on the threat to the protectee and as per the authorization in a particular category. z, strength of pso's & convoy/ carcade = depends on the threat to the protectee and as per the authorization in a particular category. y +, strength of pso's & convoy/ carcade = depends on the threat to the protectee and as per the authorization in a particular category. y, strength of pso's & convoy/ carcade = depends on the threat to the protectee and as per the authorization in a particular category. x, strength of pso's & convoy/ carcade = depends on the threat to the protectee and as per the authorization in a particular category
we may add details in a tabular form about the security admissible for these categories, as per mha guidelines.

apart from above, security is also being provided to the president of india. security of president is the responsibility of delhi police and pbg (president's body guard), which is a ceremonial guard of the president of india. it is the oldest security unit of the armed forces and senior most among all security agencies/ groups. it is working in both scenarios (in wartime as paratroopers and in peace as a ceremonial guard). it is the only cavalry unit in the world which provides security on cavalry.

metadata.subject: public_order_ethics
metadata.heading: current vip security arrangements by spg

page_content: according to the latest amendment of spg act, 1988, made in 2019 [5 th amendment] spg is mandated to provide proximate security to the pm and his immediate family members residing with him at his official residence & any former pm and his immediate family members residing with him at his official residence for a period of 5 years from the date he or she ceases to hold the office of pm.

the overall security arrangements of the vip and his immediate family members are governed by the rules and instructions compiled and documented by ib (mha) as blue book. however, various aspects of security arrangements may be broadly categorized hereunder:

1. the overall responsibility for ensuring the safety of the vip rests with the local administration within whose jurisdiction, for the time being, the vip happens to be present. however, the responsibility for providing proximate security rests with security group especially meant for the vip; 2. the concerned local police shall provide physical security arrangements; while the state/ ut police shall make all the protective arrangements. personal security will provide the proximate security; 3. the proximate security shall comprise of the provision of ring-round and escort personnel, isolation cordons, the sterile zone around the vip and the rostrum and access control to the vip at all places. 4. advance security liaison work is to be carried out jointly by officers from intelligence agencies and security agency for all programmes of the vip. 5. implementation of rules/guidelines will be the responsibility of the local police authority. it will be incumbent on all government servants, particularly, the divisional commissioners and district magistrates, the officers of all departments such as the public works department and the state electricity board, to ensure by their assistance, cooperation and support that these rules are fully implemented. 6. the tentative and final programme schedule of the vip should be well coordinated with all agencies to avoid any sort of confusion; while the organizers and the government authorities who make the programme should consult the law and order authorities for obvious advantages, the latter should also keep in close touch, on their own, with such authorities to have their views considered before the draft programme is prepared. once the programme is finalized, the chances of dropping any of the items will be very remote. any last minute change is to be strictly avoided. 7. the role of the organizers, political leaders and workers connected with vip functions is to cooperate with the police to facilitate their work and not to create any impediment that would hamper the security arrangements. security suggestions made by the police with regard to the vip visit should be given due consideration.

8. the number of dignitaries who would receive and see off the vip at the airport/ helipad or point of arrival/ departure, or those to be seated on the rostrum/ dais with the vip should be kept to the minimum and their names finalized in advance in consultation with the security agencies. 9. coordination meeting under the chairmanship of the high-level officers should be held, which will be attended by representatives of all the departments concerned with making the arrangements. such coordination meetings should also include non-official hosts, organizing parties, the heads of quasi-official bodies and the heads of local security coordinators etc., who might be associated with the visit. similar coordination meetings should also be convened at the district level to implement the general instructions and to go into further details, for the local arrangements. 10. adequate anti-terrorist planning is to be undertaken. the objective of anti- terrorist planning should be to counter both bold and desperate attacks as well as surreptitious attempts against the vip. 11. few functional briefing points viz. watching the people and not the procession or the vip, staff to continue at their posts on the route at all times, handling of crowds, supervision by senior officers, bodyguard (ring round) duties, access control, anti-sabotage precautions, purity of food and drinks, security vetting and reliability of personnel, precautions against subversion and infiltration, guarding and surveillance, vip convoy to be kept to the minimum, contingency planning etc. to be emphasized upon. 12. it is necessary to provide uninterrupted and continuous contact between the vip camp and headquarter(s). for this purpose, both wireless and telephone facilities should be made available. 13. during visits of the vip, adequate medical facilities should be provided to meet any emergent situation. 14. video and photographic record of the visit of the vip, covering the functions attended is to be ensured. this can be achieved by including a police photographer in the jeep provided for photographers during road journeys. this will enable the police photographer not only to cover such journeys, but also functions, meetings and other places visited by the vip during tours. on the conclusion of the visit, the video and photographs should be carefully studied and analyzed as part of the process of stocktaking of the security arrangements made.

metadata.subject: public_order_ethics
metadata.heading: sources of the threat at the residence/ office.

page_content: arrangements are needed to safe guard the vip while he is at his residence or temporary camp residence or at his office or any place of public or private engagement which include hotels, motels and holiday homes etc. threat to the security of the vip is likely from the following sources while the vip is in residence:

metadata.subject: public_order_ethics
metadata.heading: sources of the threat at the residence/ office.

page_content: - a) sabotage to the building and its surroundings. - b) mischief through tampering of the electrical appliances, arson, explosives and poison. - c) direct physical attack.

the authority charged with vip security should keep a plan of the intended residence and study it carefully to identify the vulnerable places which may be exploited by a potential saboteur. the underground sewerages which may facilitate access to the building, cellars and false ceiling should be thoroughly checked well before the arrival of vip to ensure that no explosive devices for sabotage are planted in them. anti sabotage check should be conducted daily preferably when the vip is away from the residence. equipment like explosive detectors should be used for conducting anti-sabotage check. the sabotage to the electrical equipment, and other appliances and tapping of phones can be prevented by getting them regularly checked by qualified technicians. the sources of water and electric supply should be checked by qualified technicians. the source of water and electric supply should be checked properly and secured alternate standby arrangements should be made in this regard it is advisable to have only one entry point to the residence and other entries should be sealed off to maintain access control to the building. no unauthorized vehicle should be allowed to be parked near the premises of the residence.

metadata.subject: public_order_ethics
metadata.heading: b) (i) measures to eliminate mischief through arson:

page_content: sometimes, mischief is caused by setting the residence of the vip on fire. proper fire safety precautions should be adopted to obviate arson. all fire fighting facilities including fire extinguishers should be kept ready.

saboteurs try to harm the vip with explosives by a few other methods also. sending explosives through mail and parcels and presents are generally employed a thorough check of the mail intended for the vip should be made with the appropriate explosive detectors to ensure that no explosives are concealed in them. similarly all packages and parcels meant for the vip should be checked thoroughly from anti-sabotage angle. no material should be allowed to be handled by the vip without employing this check.

metadata.subject: public_order_ethics
metadata.heading: iii) measures against mischief through poison:

page_content: poising of food and beverages taken by the vip, one of the methods employed by saboteur to harm the vip, is an age old practice. the assistance of a forensic laboratory is generally taken to test food poisoning.

metadata.subject: public_order_ethics
metadata.heading: c) elimination of direct physical harm:

page_content: access control is a cardinal principle in vip security. harm to the vip by direct physical attack can be prevented by protective physical security cordons at various levels at the residence (journey and public meetings also) so that strict access control is enforced they are discussed at length later in this chapter and also in part - ii, chapter - 1.

metadata.subject: public_order_ethics
metadata.heading: check list of security arrangements at camp residence

page_content: place of night halt/ camp residence for the vip should be selected keeping in view of the security requirements. following points should be kept in view for selecting the place of night halt.

metadata.subject: public_order_ethics
metadata.heading: check list of security arrangements at camp residence

page_content: - 1) place of night halt (camp residence) should have perimeter fencing/ compound wall. if these facilities are not available, the area should be suitably barricaded - 2) area around the proposed camp should be surveyed to know the various approaches to the place of halt and arrange required postings - 3) if camp residence is surrounded by thick vegetation, it should be combed and trees checked small bushy growth may be got cleared - 4) guest house/buikling plan should be studied to locate vulnerable places where sabotage can be done for eg cellars, sewerage, surroundings of the guest house/ building. - 5) patrolling parties should be arranged around the place of stay. - 6) proper lighting arrangements should be made available around the camp residence. there should be proper visibility of surrounding area during nights for observation by security personnel the lighting should not focus on the vip building - 7) all electrical supplies and electrical gadgets should be checked by electrical engineer. - 8) water supply should be checked and guarded - 9) a dy. supdt. of police should be posted as camp commandant, who should be over all incharge of security arrangements at the camp residence. he should have proper liaison with cso to vip and take appropriate security precautions.

metadata.subject: public_order_ethics
metadata.heading: check list of security arrangements at camp residence

page_content: - 10) a reception room near entry point under a reception officer (inspector) with wireless communication and men & women police to be planned, where the visitors wanting to meet vip are accommodated - 11) thorough as. check of the camp should be done including the surroundings of camp residence. - 12) as check should be conducted 24-hours in advance and guards posted, followed by final check to be completed 3-hours before the arrival of the vip - 13) immediately after the advance a.s. check two armed guards under one rsi should be posted for guarding the camp residence and strict access control arrangements enforced no unauthorized person should be allowed into the vicinity. guards should be tactical guards. - 14) additional guards may be posted depending upon the local situation and ground realities. - 15) only one entry/ exit to be operated - 16) a wicket gate (small gate) near main gate is preferable. wicket gate should be used for entry and exit of visitors. main gate should be used only for authorized vehicle movement. no unauthorized vehicle should be allowed to be parked near the camp residence - 18) no vehicle other than the vip car and if place permits the security box vehicles, fire tender and ambulance should be allowed inside the vip camp premises particularly during night. it should be ensured that the movement of the vehicles should not disturb the vip - 19) visitor's etc. should be allowed into the premises as per the directions of cso/ ps to vip - 20) list of visitors as finalized by ps/ cso the vip should be obtained and the visitors should be allowed entry into the reception room or as directed by cso to vip. - 21) dfmd/ hhmd, screening, frisking, a.s. check etc. arrangements should be made in the reception room. no item should be allowed to be taken inside the camp without checking. - 22) watchers should be posted to keep watch on the movement of suspicious and mischievous elements gaining access into the camp - 23) striking force may be kept nearby. - 24) action should be taken to ensure purity of food, water, beverages etc. supplied to the vip they should be tested by scientists of psl. - 25) list of catering personnel to be obtained and vetted and then issued duty badges 26 verification of character and antecedents of all personnel drafted in connection with the vip visit more particularly the private persons should be ensured

metadata.subject: public_order_ethics
metadata.heading: check list of security arrangements at camp residence

page_content: - 27) camp residence should also be checked for fire prevention facilities by responsible officer of the fire services department - 28) security box vehicles and fire tender with drivers should be kept available during the night - 29) contingency exit route from camp to the safe house or hospital should be identified or rehearsed in advance. - 30) anti sabotage checks and access control arrangements.

metadata.subject: public_order_ethics
metadata.heading: check list of security arrangements at camp residence

page_content: exhaustive a.s. checks and stringent access control arrangements are the corner stones of vip security. threat from human bombs and possibility of using clever refuse by various extremist groups have to be kept in mind. hence no unidentified / unchecked person should be allowed in the vicinity of vip. all police personnel posted on access control duties should be briefed emphasizing the importance of access control all security personnel posted in close proximity of vip should display pics and the uniformed personnel the duty slips. state security wing provides isolation cordon while inner cordon will be provided by the unit officers.

metadata.subject: public_order_ethics
metadata.heading: check list of security arrangements at camp residence

page_content: similarly there is potent threat to vip from explosives, electronically timed preprogrammed timer devices, remote control devices, car bombs, ieds etc. therefore thorough anti-sabotage/ anti-explosive checks should be conducted at all the places of visits of vip

metadata.subject: public_order_ethics
metadata.heading: check list of security arrangements at camp residence

page_content: for permanent residence and office, separate security schemes should be drafted and implemented keeping in view of the above points.

1. entire strength of security personnel on various duties uniformed and plain clothes at the camp residence of the vip will work under the overall supervision of camp officer. 2. camp officer will have close liaison with the cso to vip/ps to vip. 3. he should know the programmes of vip and take appropriate action accordingly. he should keep the concerned officers informed about all important matters. 4. he will brief officers and men deployed on various duties. 5. he should know the safe house and emergency hospital and contingency plan in case of emergency. 6. contingency exit route from place of night halt and also alternate route should be known to camp officer and it should be rehearsed

7. he should ensure that the supervisory officers of various duties are present at their respective postings and supervise their duties and check any slackness. he will also surprise their functioning 8. ensure that thorough a.s. check of the camp residence including surroundings of the building is conducted. 9. ensure that strict access control is enforced. 10. ensure that vehicles and drivers earmarked for emergency duties during nights are available at their locations. 11. ensure that wireless communication functions efficiently. 12. check that the wireless communication is available at the emergency hospital and safe house. 13. ensure that food and beverages supplied to vip are pure (with the assistance of fsl scientists). 14. ensure that alternate (standby) power supply is in good working order. 15. ensure that the fire fighting tenders and men are available to meet any emergency.

1. reception office should be near the main entrance/ gate. 2. r.o. should obtain list of visitors, in advance. 3. all the visitors should be directed to go first to reception office. 4. the particulars of visitors should be entered in the register kept in the reception 5. visitors should pass through dfmd and frisked. 6. their baggage should be checked and left at reception office only. 7. visitors should be made to sit in waiting room. information of the visitors should be sent to cso/ps to vip. visitors should be allowed to meet the vip only on receiving the instructions from cso/ps to vip. 8. they should be escorted to meet the vip and brought back to reception office. 9. credentials of media persons/ press photographers, etc. should also be checked. 10. the reception office should have proper haison with the cso/ps to vip and keep them informed about the visitors 11. reception officer should be armed
1. they are posted near all the doors of vip apartment/suite

2. they will be in plain clothes and armed with small concealable weapons 2. 3 isolation cordon personnel and the cso/ pso will be responsible for the proximate security of the vip. 4. they should have knowledge of the topography of the building. 5. they should not intrude on the privacy of the vip. 6. ensure that only one entry is operated 7. they should allow the visitors to the vip apartment/ suite with the consent of the cso/ pso to the vip. 8. no unauthorized entry into apartment/ suite should be allowed 9. they should know the contingency arrangements.

1. the inner cordon security will cover the area between the main gate and the vip apartment. armed guards will be at the main entrance/ gate of the vip building and the isolation cordon will be near the apartment/ suite of the vip. 2. they will be in plain clothes 3. they will be disguised as bearers or gardeners, wherever necessary. 4. they should check the loitering by the visitors. 5. they should keep watch on the people trying to enter the premises other than main entrance/gate. 6. they should be able to check any intruder trying to enter by surprise

1. they are posted at entry/ exit gates. 2. they should be armed 3. they should guard the entry/ exit gates including wicket gate. 4. they should effectively prevent the unauthorized entry into the premises and if possible open fire at intruder to avert threat to the vip. 5. they should be in uniform. during night they should wear rubber soled shoes. 6. they should also keep watch on all vulnerable points around the compound wall and prevent entry of any intruder and also look for hidden ieds. 7. they should not allow anybody on duty, without displaying their pics. they should also identify them. 8. uniformed officers must display pics on their person.

metadata.subject: public_order_ethics
metadata.heading: check list of duties of static armed guards meant for all categorised protectees

page_content: 1. the static armed guards at the residences/ offices of protected persons form the first barrier against unauthorized intrusions. these guards play a very effective check against any surprise entry into secured areas, their very presence can be a deterrent to potential assailants 2. static guards should be positioned in a well-secured place within the premises. preferably in a separate guardroom. it is essential that the standing orders for each guard are framed and explained to them. there should be an alarm system in the sentry box/ guard room, so that the sentry can alert others in an emergency. 3. important points that need to be kept in view while detailing static armed guards are:-

metadata.subject: public_order_ethics
metadata.heading: check list of duties of static armed guards meant for all categorised protectees

page_content: i). all members of the guard should be well briefed about their duties. this should be done by supervisory officers initially and subsequently during their inspection rounds.

metadata.subject: public_order_ethics
metadata.heading: check list of duties of static armed guards meant for all categorised protectees

page_content: ii). the guard should look all accessible gates and open only after the identity of the visitor is established.

metadata.subject: public_order_ethics
metadata.heading: check list of duties of static armed guards meant for all categorised protectees

page_content: iii). the guard should follow the rules regarding the tactical guard in preventing the entry of unauthorized persons into the secured premises.

metadata.subject: public_order_ethics
metadata.heading: check list of duties of static armed guards meant for all categorised protectees

page_content: iv). stand-to positions together with alternative position will be detailed for each member of the guard

metadata.subject: public_order_ethics
metadata.heading: check list of duties of static armed guards meant for all categorised protectees

page_content: v). it should be realized that an efficiently functioning guard is neither seen nor heard only exception is that in order to identify the approaching person, one member of the guard, preferably guard commander should talk to the person desirous of entering the secured premises, while the guard is in ready (to react) stand-to position, during nights.

metadata.subject: public_order_ethics
metadata.heading: check list of duties of static armed guards meant for all categorised protectees

page_content: vi). the guard should not allow any vehicle or person inside before they are checked /frisked for concealed weapons, explosives etc.

metadata.subject: public_order_ethics
metadata.heading: check list of duties of static armed guards meant for all categorised protectees

page_content: vii). the guard should rush to the aid of the pso, when required, and take measures to prevent escape of unauthorized persons, who may have managed to sneak in.

metadata.subject: public_order_ethics
metadata.heading: check list of duties of static armed guards meant for all categorised protectees

page_content: viii). all members of the guard should be well trained they should be well versed in handling their weapons and should also have knowledge of unarmed combat.

metadata.subject: public_order_ethics
metadata.heading: check list of duties of static armed guards meant for all categorised protectees

page_content: ix). the guard members should report any unusual incident or activity within their area of duty to the camp commandant and other security personnel on duty at the time.

metadata.subject: public_order_ethics
metadata.heading: check list of duties of static armed guards meant for all categorised protectees

page_content: - x). they should not indulge either in gossip with other sentries / staff during duty hours or in loose talk during off duty hours about the protected person's routine etc - xi). they should have knowledge of the local language

1. static guards may not be provided in cases, where the protected person happens to stay in a multi- storeyed flat or in a private residence, where there is no space for location of a guard room. in lieu of the uniformed guards, required number of plain clothed security personnel with concealed weapons will be deployed. further static guards shall not be housed in tents/ temporary structures erected in road margins and public paths. 2. during the visits of the protected persons to the public places like hotels, restaurants and parks etc. only plain clothes psos should be allowed to accompany the protectee inside the place and no uniformed armed men should be deployed except for president vice president and prime minister of india.

1. the objective of posting watchers is to forewarn the security men of impending danger. 2. the watchers are normally unarmed 3. the watchers should make themselves inconspicuous while on duty. 4. the watchers should have knowledge of family members and friends of the protectee (list of frequent visitors noted at the end of the watchers note book.) 5. the watchers should have good rapport with the security officers and men at the residence of the protectee 6. the watchers should post themselves in the proximity of the protected person's residence. 7. the watcher should act as a scout to survey the area in advance and forewarn the security personnel about presence of any suspicious person, suspicious vehicle, presence of suspicious item around vip residence. 8. the watcher should patrol the vicinity of the protectee's residence and observe the surroundings. 9. the watcher should bear in mind that they are not out of view of the security men. 10. the watcher should keenly observe the activities, movements of the people in the vicinity and surroundings of the residence so that any

- unusual movement, is properly communicated to the concerned security officer/ personnel 11. the watchers are supposed to be in constant observation. instant analysis and in communication with the concerned security officer to maintain peak efficiency. 12. they should maintain 'watchers note book and write in it the time of relief or important events if any. the checking officer should sign in the note book.

metadata.subject: public_order_ethics
metadata.heading: threats from following sources to be eliminated for safe railway journey of vip.

page_content: - i i) ) - sabotage to permanent way - i ii i) ) tampering with signals - i ii ii i) ) sabotage to the vip coach - i iv v) ) crowds at halting stations - v v) ) mischief from food and drinks - v vi i) ) access to vip by unauthorized persons - a) sabotage to permanent way - 1) rail tracks are easily amenable to sabotage - 2) track should be divided into segments for convenience of checking - 3) close co-ordination between police and railway administration is very important. joint teams of railway & police officials should be organized to check and guard the track/ segment - 4) special attention should be paid to culverts, bridges besides the rail track for explosives - 5) track should be inspected for its operational reliability - 6) area of about 10 mtrs on either side of the track should be checked - 7) traces of recent diggings, freshly dug earth should be looked for. - 8) detailed advance search and checks should be done in daylight only. - 9) final checking to be completed 2 hours before the scheduled time - 10) after a.s. check, posting of police at vulnerable points particularly level crossings, culverts, bridges should be done immediately.
important signal systems should be manned by senior railway officials along with police officials, to avoid tampering which may result in a major disaster.

- 1) special coach to be checked one hour before the occupation of the vip and kept under guard till the vip occupies - 2) no inflammable / explosive materials should be taken on board - 3) special attention should be paid to the carriages adjoining the vip coach. - 4) all electrical and mechanical devices to be tested to rule out tampering - 5) thorough a.s. check of the coach should be done well in advance
- 1) uniformed men should be deployed for controlling the crowds gathered to see/meet the vip - 2) plain clothes men should be posted for watching suspected persons among the crowds
purity of food and drinks should be ensured

- 1) govt. railway police should depute an armed escort of 1 hc & 4 pcs under one sl who will travel in the adjoining compartment of the vip. - 2) when the train halts, depending upon the crowds, one pc of escort party shall take position near the vip compartment and keep a careful watch. - 3) another pc shall take his position on the opposite side of the compartment. - 4) they must guard against any unauthorized person trying to gain access to the compartment. - 5) when the train is about to leave, they will get back to their compartment. - 6) railway police on duty at the halting station will be responsible for security of vip. senior most railway police official present at the time at the railway station should supervise the security arrangements. - 7) personal security staff of vip will remain alert and keep a careful watch on the front and rear doors.
threats from the following sources has to be eliminated.

metadata.subject: public_order_ethics
metadata.heading: a) sabotage to air strip/ helipad

page_content: 1. perimeter security of air strip 2. access control system enforced 3. elevated surrounding areas should be checked and guarded 4. runway cleared of all impediments 5. a.s. check of the runway jointly by police and airport authorities before landing or take off of the vip aircraft 6. vip aircraft to be received or stationed at an exclusive area 7. detailed security arrangements are given under "security arrangements at helipad in chapter iii".

metadata.subject: public_order_ethics
metadata.heading: a) sabotage to air strip/ helipad

page_content: note:

metadata.subject: public_order_ethics
metadata.heading: a) sabotage to air strip/ helipad

page_content: helipad arrangements for the visit of president, vice

metadata.subject: public_order_ethics
metadata.heading: a) sabotage to air strip/ helipad

page_content: president and prime minister of india is given in chapter xvi

1. thorough a.s. check of the air craft should be conducted by aeronautical engineer and its air worthiness must be certified 2. joint a.s. check of air craft by the engineer and police officer an hour before the occupation by the vip 3. after the check, air craft should be kept under guard 4. purity of fuel and oil for the air craft should be ensured test samples taken - c) food and drinks:
purity of food should be ensured
- 1) luggage must be checked for explosive and inflammable articles - 2) unchecked articles should not be taken on board

metadata.subject: public_order_ethics
metadata.heading: e) threat from co-passengers in commercial flights:

page_content: two well-trained security officers should follow the vip in the commercial flights to intercept and take care of mischievous elements in case of need

metadata.subject: public_order_ethics
metadata.heading: f) crowds at the airport/ helipads

page_content: 1. crowds to be controlled 2. adequate uniformed and plain clothes men should be posted to control the crowd

metadata.subject: public_order_ethics
metadata.heading: f) crowds at the airport/ helipads

page_content: 3. all elevations, visitors gallery should be taken care of by posting uniformed and plain clothed police men only those dignitaries, whose names have been finalised earlier, should be allowed to receive the vip.

metadata.subject: public_order_ethics
metadata.heading: f) crowds at the airport/ helipads

page_content: n.b. detailed instructions given under security arrangements at helipad should be implemented

metadata.subject: public_order_ethics
metadata.heading: precautions to be observed regarding use of state owned/ private owned aircraft/ helicopters carrying political leaders/ vips.

page_content: in view of the increased usage of privately owned small aircrafts/helicopters by leaders of political parties/vips for visits, electioneering or other purposes and keeping in mind some unfortunate accidents that have taken place in the last five years, precautionary measures are suggested for observance for vips in the use of such aircrafts/helicopters.

metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

1. vital installations like control tower, radar etc. should be guarded 2. movement of the vehicles should be strictly checked at the time of arrival and take off 3. monitoring of the progress of the vip air craft should be done till it reaches its next destination.

- 1) mechanical reliability of the water craft/ boat should be certified by the com petent authority - 2) a.s. check to be conducted - 3) vip cabin to be thoroughly searched - 4) luggage should be checked - 5) purity of fuel and oil ensured - 6) test samples taken
separate boat for escort under police (escort) officer with, buoys, ropes, first aid box with wireless communication should follow the vip boat.
1. the crew should have professional competency 2. verification of charter and antecedents should be ensured.

1. baggage to be checked 2. crowd control arrangements must be made at embarking and disembarking points 3. life saving devices like life jacket etc. should be arranged on board 4. expert swimmers must be available on board 5. medical communication, fire fighting arrangements should be ensured on board

1. when moving among crowds. int., sw will form isolation cordon around the vip. the district ring round team / second escort party should form inner security cordon/ ring and prevent rush of crowds towards vip. they should keep watch on the movements of the people around the vip to spot possible trouble. 2. rope parties may be arranged depending on the requirement. 3. garlands, presentations etc. should be checked before hand 4. people should be presented to the vip in an orderly manner for representation of their grievances or submission of memorandum etc. 5. more careful while passing bushes, trees, fast moving vehicles.

in respect of most popular and highly threatened vips (2+ category), the cpt (close protection team) of the vip, will perform security duties by moving in the shape of a diamond "a as per the diagram below.
1. movement in diamond formation (by cpt)-
the area inside the diamond formation is a sterile zone.
district security (e-ii/r.r. team) will take the position of inner security cordon behind the c.p.t.

- a) establish early liaison with the principal host. - b) find out plans and arrangements for the function. - c) politely suggest modification that could enhance security. - d) do not come out with un-reasonable suggestions, as far as possible. - e) suggestions should be made keeping in view the conventions, the tradition of the function for eg. convocation.

- a) visit the place of function well in advance and plan the security arrangements. - b) a. s. check may be conducted wherever mandatory. - c) depending upon the function and wherever necessary vetting of servicing personnel purity of food and drinks should be ensured and medical facilities/ fire fighting arrangements be arranged. - d) wherever necessary, system of invitation cards for admission to the function may be suggested and enforced

- e) wherever necessary, cards to be checked at the gate by the representatives of the host, with the assistance of the security officials. - f) outside the place of function, uniformed arrangements and traffic arrangements may be made.

- a) deploy plain cloth officers in a function to interrupt threat. - b) they should be suitably dressed for the occasion. - c) officers should behave naturally to invitees and watch for suspicious strangers. - d) presentations usually should be checked in advance. - e) only seasoned and experienced officers should be detailed for these duties.

at times, vip may attend functions arranged in pandals/ structures erected temporarily. as these are temporary, there is possibility of collapsing or catching fire. there are incidents in which the persons attending the function in a temporary structure, particularly children and women have succumbed to the burn injuries. the structure caught fire due to electrical short circuit and there was very little opening to escape.

therefore, it is essential that the temporary structures/ pandals are erected taking into all considerations such electrical fittings, firefighting arrangements, escape routes, strongness of structure.

in this connection ib, mha has communicated a circular (annexure-23) to all the states. instructions contained in the circular should be strictly adhered to. presence, supervision and role of concerned police as highlighted in the construction of dais should be followed, keeping in view the possibility of planting ieds etc. during the course of erection of temporary structure/ pandals, should be strictly followed.

1. rostrum check means checking the place or stage or dias, from where the vip speaks with the public. 2. a certificate from r&b department on the capacity and standard of the stage/dias shall be obtained. 3. persons permitted by the collector and sp alone will be allowed on the dias. 4. a way with steps for the to and from movement of vip to the dais shall be left free. 5. in front of the stage d-zone with 50 to 70 feet vacant area shall be formed. 6. the rostrum, garlands, bouquets, electronic mikes, fans, air coolers, water bottles, and flower pots shall be thoroughly checked, well in advance, before the arrival of the vip/. (to be sanitized with dfmd,ncjd & doy). 7. the strength of rostrum can also be checked in advanced (d-24) by positioning required people on the rostrum physically (not to be rely only on r & b certificates)

page_content: 1. a responsible police officer of the rank of dsp should be incharge of alighting/ departure points. he should know that the alighting and departure points are extremely vulnerable for an attack on vip. 2. wherever necessary 'view cutters should be arranged at alighting/ boarding points. 3. no unauthorized and unchecked persons should be allowed anywhere near the alighting point. 4. sterile zone should be created at the alighting point. 5. only the members of the reception committee whose names have been cleared (in the asl) and officials (with duty passes for alighting point) should be allowed near the alighting point. 6. to ensure that all the reception members/ introduces should be checked and advised to lineup. they should be orderly and not jostle around 7. bouquets, garlands and other presentations at the alighting point should be checked by asc team in advance. 8. one plain clothes hc to be posted at all places of halt for the security of the vip car from the time of arrival, till the carcade leaves. 9. rope parties may be kept ready for use. 10. sufficient strength of striking force should be kept ready nearby.

page_content: in the year 2004, government of composite ap state invited cpi (ml) maoist for talks after lifting ban. discussions were held between government representatives and leaders of maoists led by the then state committee secretary. however, the talks failed and ban was reimposed. in subsequent years, the party suffered heavy losses as many of its top leaders were killed in exchange of fire with security forces in telangana area. in the year 2010, the central committee of the party issued a circular directing all squads and ug cadre to retreat to danakaranya zone, as a tactical move to preserve the cadre, as people were not cooperating them by way of giving shelter and food and security forces were successively eliminating them in encounters. by the end of that year all the cadre withdrew to dk zone. since then many a time, they tried to re enter into the border areas of telangana area to retrieve the lost ground, but had to beat a hasty retreat to dk area as security forces chased them and killed some of their armed squads' embers in exchange of fire. after basvaraju took over as general secretary of the party, the cc reorganised its divisional committes and area commities of telangana state and in 2019, resolved to send dvcs and acs into operational areas in ts. the state committee in the year 2021 reviewed their re-entry move into ts and decided that movement of military formations in the state is not possible as security forces were alert. hence it (cc) decided in future to send only 1+3 teams in mufti without weapons only for a couple of months and retreat. but that move also is not put into action as security forces are vigilant.

metadata.subject: public_order_ethics
metadata.heading: counter strategy for maoists - plan of action

page_content: - district special parties should be strengthened and imparted training by grey hounds. - proper surveillance should be maintained at border crossing points (of maoists) and villages that are bordering to chathisgarh and maharasra states. - constant vehicle checking should be conducted for ug cadres and for fire arms and explosive material. - village visiting should be held much frequently to know the movements of extremists as also for sympathisers who are likely to provide shelter and food to cadre. - community policing should be encouraged to win over the hearts of the people by involving youth and other communities. - survillence on militants, sympathisers, ex-cadre and overt activists should be intensified. - attendence at police stations should be organised for miltants, sympathisers and ex-cadre during run up to general elections-2024. - proper cases should be registered against the members of frontal and mass organisations of maoists whenever they indulge in support activities. - anti-maoist propaganda should be done in systematic manner involving print, electronic and social media. - area domination exercises should be done with the frequent movement of security forces in affected areas. police visibility in the extremists' affected areas will instill confidence among the people. it also acts as deterrence to maoist squads and action teams. - road opening parties (rop) should be pressed into service for antisabotage checks of culverts and vulnerable points on the roads for detection and prevention of land mines planted by maoists. - opening of armed out posts in the interior extremists' affected areas. - fool proof security measures should be taken for the protection of police stations and aops - protecting the targets and potential targets should be the top priority of police. - combing operations should be conducted to neutralise the maoist squads and action teams by collecting intelligence on their movements. - counter action teams consisting of selected, competent officers should be formed to identify and neutralise the maoist action teams.

metadata.subject: public_order_ethics
metadata.heading: counter strategy for maoists - plan of action

page_content: - hard core militants and sympathisers should be identified for booking them under ap public security act.

- collection of intelligence about extremists is very much needed to prevent offences committed by them as well as in neutralising their rank and file (cadre). - source building plays crucial role in collecting intelligence about them. source building involves three stages i.e. identification of source, converting the source, so identified, as police informant and cultivating him/ her on long term basis. - identification of source-any person who has access to information can only share that information. usually sympathisers and militants, who provide shelter and food to uc cadre, can fit into this role. members of squads, couriers and other ug cadre have much more access to information about the exact movements and activities of squads and senior uc cadre. - converting as informant-such identified person may be lured by offering hefty money. sometimes vexed with changes in policies of the party or excessive violent actions, some among the identified persons become disgruntled and may be ready to provide intelligence. such persons can be converted as informant. - cultivating him/her-once he/ she becomes informant, the information passed on by them is most likely to lead to location of squad or ug cadre resulting in the arrest or neutralisation. two crucial factors in these deals are confidentiality against exposure and keeping the promise made.
